subtitle: From the burning salai of Kandalur to the fallen throne of Anuradhapura

panels.shot: epic low-angle wide of a warship prowed with a tiger emblem cutting through dark waves toward a distant island city
panels.action: Rajaraja Chola I stands at the prow of a Chola warship, cloak and hair blown by sea wind, hand resting on the hilt of his sword as the outline of Anuradhapura rises on the horizon under a brooding sky.

dialogue.text: Rajaraja Chola I, lord of the Kaveri, bears his sword from Kandalur’s burning salai to the ancient throne of Lanka in the age of rising empires.

i18n.hi: कावेरी का स्वामी राजराज चोल प्रथम, कांडलूर की जली सलै से लंका के प्राचीन सिंहासन तक तलवार लिए बढ़ा, जब साम्राज्य उठ रहे थे।

panels.shot: wide interior of a dim pillared hall in Thanjavur, lit by oil lamps, with maps spread before a king
panels.action: A younger Rajaraja Chola I leans over a palm-leaf map in the council hall of Thanjavur, his face calm but intent, while flickering lamps throw long shadows on pillars carved with tigers and lotus motifs.

dialogue.text: In the late tenth century, the Kaveri valley glowed with lamps, temples, and trade. Yet Rajaraja, newly crowned in Thanjavur, saw beyond its river fields to seas and rival crowns.

i18n.hi: दसवीं सदी के अंत में कावेरी घाटी दीपों, मंदिरों और बाज़ारों से दमकती थी। पर तंजावूर में नया ताज पहन कर राजराज की नज़र नदी से आगे, समंदर और दुश्मन ताजों पर थी।

panels.shot: medium close-up of Rajaraja in council, profile view, with distant sea imagined behind him
panels.action: Rajaraja Chola I sits upright on a low throne, councillors blurred behind him, as his gaze seems to look past the hall walls toward an imagined coastline, the faint outline of ships suggested in the background haze.

dialogue.text: To the west lay the Chera’s coast and their famed salai of Kandalur, a fortified seat of warriors and ships. To the south, the old Pandya crown smouldered, backed by friends across the sea in Lanka.

i18n.hi: पश्चिम में चेऱों का समुद्रतट था, उनकी किलेबंद कांडलूर सलै, योद्धाओं और जहाज़ों का अड्डा। दक्षिण में पुराना पांड्य ताज धधक रहा था, जिसके पीछे लंका के साथी खड़े थे।

panels.shot: sweeping bird’s-eye view of the Kaveri delta shipyards with the king inspecting
panels.action: On a muddy riverbank near the Kaveri, rows of long wooden hulls lie half-built as carpenters work; Rajaraja Chola I rides past on horseback, pointing toward the river, while overseers bow and scribes note details.

dialogue.text: Rajaraja did not rush to war. He counted land, grain, and men, reformed his levies, and turned river craftsmen to the building of ships. The Chola tiger would learn to swim.

i18n.hi: राजराज ने जल्दबाज़ी में युद्ध नहीं छेड़ा। उसने ज़मीन, अन्न, आदमी गिने, सेना सुधारी, और नदी के कारीगरों से जहाज़ बनवाए। चोल शेर ने तैरना सीख लिया।

panels.shot: low-angle wide of Kandalur coastline with Chola fleet offshore at dawn
panels.action: At a misty dawn off a rugged Kerala coast, sleek Chola vessels lie offshore under low clouds, their masts dark silhouettes as waves crash against the rocky shoreline where the distant complex of Kandalur Salai sits guarded.

dialogue.text: His meykkirti would later boast: he destroyed Kandalur Salai, where the Chera once fed their warriors. Before the temple-fed warriors could march, Chola ships and swords crept upon their coast.

i18n.hi: बाद में उसकी मय्यकिर्ति ने शेखी बघारी: उसने कांडलूर सलै तोड़ी, जहाँ चेऱ योद्धाओं को खिलाते थे। मंदिर के पलटे उठते उससे पहले, चोल जहाज़ और तलवारें उनके तट पर रेंग आईं।

panels.shot: dynamic mid-shot of Rajaraja leading troops through smoke toward the salai
panels.action: Rajaraja Chola I strides in the forefront of mailed infantry through drifting smoke, his sword lowered at his side, as behind him Chola soldiers surge toward a fortified complex of halls and granaries on fire in the distance.

dialogue.text: Kandalur’s salai fell. Its halls, once rich with gifts of kings, now cracked with flame. With that blow, Rajaraja drove a wedge into Chera pride and claimed the western sea for his fleets.

i18n.hi: कांडलूर की सलै ढह गई। राजाओं की भेंटों से भरे उसके सभागृह अब आग से चटक रहे थे। इस वार से राजराज ने चेऱ अभिमान चीर दिया और पश्चिमी समंदर अपने बेड़ों के नाम कर दिया।

panels.shot: medium shot in a quiet camp at night with Rajaraja seated by a lamp, map of the south laid out
panels.action: Under a night sky in a field camp, Rajaraja Chola I sits cross-legged by a low lamp inside his pavilion, fingers resting on a palm-leaf map showing Madurai and the southern marches, while distant torches mark the sleeping army.

dialogue.text: From Kandalur, his gaze turned inward again. The ancient Pandya land, weakened but proud, leaned on Lankan kings and their gold. The Chola saw that to secure his river, he must steady the south.

i18n.hi: कांडलूर के बाद उसकी नज़र फिर भीतर मुड़ी। प्राचीन पांड्य भूमि, कमज़ोर पर अक्खड़, लंका के राजाओं और उनके सोने पर टिके थी। चोल ने समझा, नदी बचानी हो तो दक्षिण संभालना होगा।

panels.shot: epic wide shot of Chola army advancing toward a distant city representing Madurai
panels.action: Across a dusty plain, banners with the Chola tiger ripple above rows of spearmen and cavalry advancing in ordered ranks toward the distant towers and temple gopuram of a city standing on the horizon.

dialogue.text: Rajaraja marched on the Pandyas. His inscriptions remember how he took their crowns and slew their power, binding their land as feudatories. Each captured banner was another knot in his growing net of order.

i18n.hi: राजराज पांड्यों पर चढ़ आया। उसके शिलालेख बताते हैं कैसे उसने उनके ताज छीने, ताकत तोड़ी, और उनकी धरती को अधीन बना बाँध लिया। हर झंडा उसके बढ़ते जाल में एक और गिरह बना।

panels.shot: medium shot inside a captured Pandya hall, with Rajaraja receiving submission
panels.action: In a pillared hall adorned with fading fish emblems of the Pandyas, a defeated local chieftain kneels with folded hands before Rajaraja Chola I, who sits composed on a low throne, a scribe beside him recording the grant.

dialogue.text: Yet he did not merely break. He bound. Grants were written, temples endowed, officers installed. The Chola system reached into village and shrine, tightening the weave of authority over the southern plains.

i18n.hi: पर वह केवल तोड़ने नहीं, जोड़ने भी निकला। दानपत्र लिखे गए, मंदिरों को संपत्ति मिली, अधिकारी बिठाए गए। चोल व्यवस्था गाँव और देवालय तक पहुँची, दक्षिण के मैदानों पर सत्ता की बुनाई कसती गई।

panels.shot: wide shot of a coastal port with Chola fleet being loaded, Lanka visible as a faint silhouette
panels.action: At a bustling eastern port, long piers stretch into the sea as soldiers, horses, and supplies file up planks into Chola ships, while across the faint blue line of the strait, a hazy outline hints at the distant island of Lanka.

dialogue.text: Beyond the southern cape lay Lanka, where the Anuradhapura kings had long tangled in Pandya affairs. In time, Rajaraja turned his new navy and hardened legions toward that island of ancient monasteries and wealth.

i18n.hi: दक्षिणी छोर के पार थी लंका, जहाँ अनुराधपुर के राजा अरसे से पांड्य मामलों में उलझे थे। समय आया तो राजराज ने नई नौसेना और सख़्त सेना उस प्राचीन मठों और धन की द्वीप पर मोड़ी।

panels.shot: dramatic low-angle of Chola ships mid-sea in rough waters
panels.action: Chola warships, oars cutting rhythmically, ride heavy swells under a lowering sky; at the prow of the lead vessel, Rajaraja Chola I stands braced, cloak pressed against him by spray as distant lightning flickers.

dialogue.text: It was a bold thing, to carry war over the sea. But the strait between Kaveri and Lanka narrowed under his will. His meykkirti would later proclaim: he took Ilam, seizing the long-renowned Anuradhapura.

i18n.hi: समंदर पार युद्ध ले जाना बड़ा दुस्साहस था। पर कावेरी और लंका के बीच की खाड़ी उसकी इच्छा से सिमट गई। उसकी मय्यकिर्ति ने कहा: उसने ईलम लिया, कीर्ति–गाथा अनुराधपुर पर कब्ज़ा किया।

panels.shot: wide shot of Chola troops landing on Lanka’s shore and advancing inland
panels.action: On a sandy Sri Lankan beach lined with palms, Chola soldiers disembark from beached ships, forming ranks as they move off the shore toward the green interior, while Rajaraja Chola I confers with officers near the waterline.

dialogue.text: The Lankan chronicles, in their own tongue, speak of foreign hosts and burning shrines. Between Chola record and island memory, one truth stands clear: a great capital was taken, and the island’s order was broken.

i18n.hi: लंका की अपनी कथाएँ कहती हैं पराए सेनिकों और जलते देवालयों की। चोल लेख और द्वीप की याद के बीच एक बात साफ़ है: एक महान राजधानी गिरी, और द्वीप की व्यवस्था टूट गई।

panels.shot: epic wide of Anuradhapura’s ruined city with Chola banners rising
panels.action: Amid toppled walls and smoke-wreathed stupas of Anuradhapura, Chola standards bearing the tiger are raised over a palace courtyard, while Rajaraja Chola I stands in the foreground, grave and still, taking in the fallen city.

dialogue.text: Anuradhapura, seat of centuries of Lankan kings, fell to his hand. Its royal line fled, its treasures and sacred relics were seized or scattered. The island’s balance tilted toward the Kaveri king.

i18n.hi: अनुराधपुर, सदियों से लंका के राजाओं की गद्दी, उसके हाथ में आ गया। राजवंश भाग खड़ा हुआ, खज़ाने और पवित्र अवशेष या तो लूटे गए या बिखर गए। द्वीप का संतुलन कावेरी के राजा की ओर झुक गया।

panels.shot: medium interior of a commandeered Lankan hall with Rajaraja issuing orders to administrators
panels.action: Inside a commandeered hall with Lankan carvings, Rajaraja Chola I stands over seated scribes as they inscribe orders, while a local official and a Chola commander listen; outside, a glimpse of soldiers patrolling tight streets.

dialogue.text: Victory brought another task: rule. Garrisons were placed, taxes assessed, shrines both controlled and endowed. Rajaraja’s rule in Lanka was firm but not whole, held through force, revenue, and the sword’s shadow.

i18n.hi: जीत के साथ एक और काम आया: राज चलाना। किलों में फ़ौजें बिठीं, कर बाँटे गए, देवालयों को काबू भी किया गया, दान भी। लंका में राजराज की पकड़ सख़्त थी, पर अधूरी, तलवार, लगान और डर पर टिकी।

panels.shot: medium shot of Rajaraja back in Thanjavur, overseeing temple foundations (Brihadisvara in early stages)
panels.action: Back in Thanjavur, Rajaraja Chola I stands barefoot on a plinth as huge dressed stones are hauled into place by teams of workers, the early mass of the Brihadisvara temple rising behind him under the bright Kaveri sun.

dialogue.text: His sword crossed seas, but its gains flowed into stone and order at home. Land from Pandya and Lanka fed endowments. In Thanjavur, the great temple rose, its lamps fuelled by conquered fields and ports.

i18n.hi: उसकी तलवार ने समंदर चीर दिए, पर जीत की धारा पत्थर और व्यवस्था में घर लौटी। पांड्य और लंका की ज़मीन दान में गई। तंजावूर में महा–मंदिर उठा, जिसके दीप जीते खेतों और बंदरों के तेल से जले।

panels.shot: close-up on an inscription being carved, Rajaraja’s figure faint in the background
panels.action: A stonecutter’s chisel bites into granite, carving neat Tamil letters that record Rajaraja’s titles and conquests, while, blurred in the background, the king watches from a verandah with his hands clasped behind his back.

dialogue.text: In his meykkirtis, he had the wars named: Kandalur Salai burned, Pandyas subdued, Ilam taken. But the same walls spoke of gifts, weights, fields, and regulations. Power was set down as record, not only as boast.

i18n.hi: अपनी मय्यकिर्तियों में उसने युद्धों के नाम खुदवाए: कांडलूर सलै जली, पांड्य झुके, ईलम जीता। पर वही दीवारें दान, तोल, खेत और नियम भी बोलती थीं। शक्ति सिर्फ़ शेखी नहीं, लेख बनकर दर्ज हुई।

panels.shot: medium shot of Rajaraja in a quieter old age, overlooking the Kaveri from a balcony at dusk
panels.action: An older Rajaraja Chola I, hair streaked with grey, stands at a palace balcony at dusk, looking out over the branching channels of the Kaveri, boats and fields glowing in the last light, his posture reflective.

dialogue.speaker: rajaraja_chola_i
dialogue.text: Let the seas know our strength, and our villages know our justice.

i18n.hi: समंदर हमारी ताकत जानें, और गाँव हमारा न्याय।

panels.shot: epic high-angle montage of the Chola world: Kaveri temples, Kerala coast, and Lanka, linked by sea routes
panels.action: A sweeping composite view shows the tall vimana of Thanjavur, the rocky Kerala coast near Kandalur, and the ruins and stupas of Anuradhapura, all connected by silver lines of sea and trade dotted with tiny Chola ships.

dialogue.text: Rajaraja’s age tied river, coast, and island into one imperial web. His sword humbled Kandalur, Pandya, and Lanka—but his lasting mark lay in the order he laid across the waters and lands of the south.

i18n.hi: राजराज का युग नदी, तट और द्वीप को एक साम्राज्यिक जाल में पिरो गया। उसकी तलवार ने कांडलूर, पांड्य और लंका को झुकाया — पर उसकी असली छाप उस व्यवस्था में है जो उसने दक्षिण की धरती और जल पर बिछाई।

title_i18n.hi: राजराज की तलवार: कांडलूर से लंका तक

subtitle_i18n.en: From the burning salai of Kandalur to the fallen throne of Anuradhapura
subtitle_i18n.hi: कांडलूर की जलती सलै से अनुराधपुर के टूटे सिंहासन तक
